rationale: The citing paper gives a specific account of Khan and Jain (2005), noting their empirical finding that combining nonlinear pricing and store-level pricing can increase retailer profitability. It then explicitly uses that study as a contrast point to define its own contribution: moving from a monopolistic retailer focus to manufacturers' nonlinear pricing incentives in a competitive setting. This is more than a passing citation list entry because it helps frame the research gap and positioning of the 2010 paper.
relation_description: The 2010 paper builds on Khan and Jain (2005) as prior empirical evidence on nonlinear pricing profitability, then extends the line of inquiry to a different strategic context (competitive manufacturers rather than a monopolistic retailer). The cited study is used to motivate and delimit the new contribution rather than to dispute it.
